why_it_fits: A broad proxy for recurring household spend, especially groceries, everyday goods, and pharmacy-led traffic.
risk_watch: Low-margin retail; mix shifts and consumer trade-down can change profit capture even if basket demand stays firm.

why_it_fits: Direct proxy for one of the biggest current human-goods lines: pharmaceutical and medical-product consumption.
risk_watch: Concentration in a few blockbuster franchises and policy risk around drug pricing.

business_focus: Packaged food, beverages, nutrition, pet care
why_it_fits: A diversified way to express everyday food-and-beverage demand, with additional exposure to nutrition and pet categories.
risk_watch: FX, coffee/cocoa costs, and slower discretionary food categories.

why_it_fits: Another direct proxy for the large and growing pharmaceutical component of the human consumption basket.
risk_watch: Patent cliffs, pipeline execution, and reimbursement pressure.

why_it_fits: The clearest listed proxy for the single largest silicon-basket bucket: high-end accelerators and AI-system demand.
risk_watch: Customer concentration, export controls, and the pace of custom-ASIC substitution.

business_focus: Custom AI accelerators, AI networking, connectivity
why_it_fits: Strong exposure to two fast-growing silicon needs at once: custom accelerators and AI networking/interconnect.
risk_watch: AI program concentration among a small number of hyperscale buyers.

headline_fact: 2025 revenue: NT$3,809.05B; advanced nodes 77% of Q4 wafer revenue
why_it_fits: A core manufacturing bottleneck for the compute-heavy side of the silicon basket, especially leading-edge AI chips.
risk_watch: Geopolitics, capex intensity, and cyclicality outside AI.

why_it_fits: Direct exposure to the fastest-growing memory layer in the silicon basket: HBM attached to AI accelerators.
risk_watch: Memory-price cyclicality and execution on next-generation HBM qualification.
